response:
**FOUNDATIONS OF TRUST**  
COMMUNITY BUILDING BEGINS WITH TRUST-ESTABLISHING RELATIONSHIPS THROUGH HONESTY-RESPECT-AND CONSISTENCY-TRUST IS FOSTERED WHEN ACTIONS ALIGN WITH WORDS-AND WHEN LEADERS SHOW VULNERABILITY-AND EMPATHY-THE PASTORAL ROLE DEMANDS LISTENING MORE THAN SPEAKING-TO UNDERSTAND THE NEEDS OF THE CONGREGATION-AND TO CREATE A SAFE SPACE FOR ALL TO BELONG-TRUST IS THE BEDROCK OF ANY STRONG COMMUNITY-IT CANNOT BE FORCED-IT MUST BE CULTIVATED-DAY BY DAY-AND PERSON BY PERSON.  

BUILDING TRUST REQUIRES INTENTIONAL EFFORT-AND A COMMITMENT TO TRANSPARENCY-AND ACCOUNTABILITY-LEADERS MUST BE HONEST ABOUT STRUGGLES-AND SUCCESS-TO MODEL INTEGRITY-AND TO ENCOURAGE OPEN COMMUNICATION-THIS CREATES AN ENVIRONMENT WHERE MEMBERS FEEL VALUED-AND HEARD-AND MOTIVATED TO CONTRIBUTE TO THE WHOLE-TRUST ALSO INVOLVES RECOGNIZING DIVERSITY-AND HONORING DIFFERENT VOICES-TO ENSURE THAT EVERYONE FEELS INCLUDED-AND RESPECTED-IT IS THROUGH THIS FOUNDATION THAT A TRUE COMMUNITY CAN EMERGE-AND THRIVE.  

**PRACTICES OF INCLUSION**  
INCLUSION IS ESSENTIAL FOR A HEALTHY COMMUNITY-IT INVOLVES RECOGNIZING THAT EVERY INDIVIDUAL HAS A UNIQUE ROLE-AND VALUE-TOGETHER-PEOPLE CAN CONTRIBUTE TO A GREATER GOOD-PASTORAL LEADERS MUST CREATE OPPORTUNITIES FOR EVERYONE TO PARTICIPATE-AND TO LEAD-REGARDLESS OF THEIR BACKGROUND-OR STATUS-INCLUDING ALL MEMBERS FOSTERS UNITY-AND A SENSE OF BELONGING-IT ALSO INVOLVES ACTIVELY SEEKING OUT THOSE WHO MAY FEEL MARGINALIZED-AND INVITING THEM INTO THE CENTER-OF COMMUNITY LIFE.  

PRACTICES OF INCLUSION CAN TAKE MANY FORMS-INCLUDING SMALL GROUPS-AND SERVICE OUTREACH-AND WORSHIP THAT REFLECTS DIVERSITY-THROUGH THESE ACTIVITIES-PEOPLE BUILD CONNECTIONS-AND STRENGTHEN BONDS-IT
